page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, with 4.0 GB of memory allocated per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance is suited for moderate workloads, though it exhibits limitations under high-intensity compute scenarios. The Intel Xeon processor supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets, contributing to average performance in PassMark CPU and extended instruction tests. However, stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks fall into the lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput for large block read and write operations, but small block cached reads are relatively weak.

In application-specific benchmarks, the instance demonstrates weak performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. Large-model LLM inference is also constrained, with poor prompt processing and text generation speeds on 7B models, though it achieves average speeds on smaller 135M models. Given these characteristics, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for legacy enterprise applications, development environments, and general-purpose workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high-performance storage or extreme computational throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on a KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results position the instance in the average tier for PassMark CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, while single-core and multi-core stress-ng performance remains weak. Application benchmarks reveal limitations in high-throughput Redis operations and static web serving. Consequently, this instance is best suited for moderate general-purpose workloads, development environments, and legacy enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring high-performance storage or specialized accelerators.
